_meta.description: Phase 2 data patch for joy registry 097 Part 3: 7 Greek joy terms from word_joy_Part3.md. Includes 1 XREF cross-reference record for kauchaomai (registry 123). Completes Phase 2 for joy registry 097.

rows.root_families_in_prior_parts: SIMCHAH_ROOT, SASOWN_ROOT, GIL_ROOT, CHEDVAH_ROOT, SALAD_ROOT, RANAN_ROOT, MAVLIGIT_ROOT, HEDAD_ROOT, TSAVACH_ROOT

rows.meaning: delight, great joy; exultation, extreme joy
rows.lsj_entry: agalliasis, eos, he: great joy, exultation [LXX+NT+3rd c.AD+]

rows.meaning: joy, gladness, cheerfulness; joy, gladness, rejoicing, Acts 2:28; 14:17
rows.lsj_entry: eufrosune: (euphron) mirth, merriment; especially of a banquet, good cheer, festivity [8th c.BC+]; also in later Prose [LXX+NT+4th c.BC+]; eufrosune psuches oinos pinomenos [LXX]. II one of the Graces [8th c.BC+]
rows.status_note: Act 2:28 quotes Psa 16:11 LXX; God makes full of gladness with his presence - god_as_subject from this citation.

rows.meaning: joy, rejoicing, happiness, gladness; joy, gladness, rejoicing, Mt. 2:10; 13:20, 44; 28:8; meton, joy, cause of joy, occasion of rejoicing, Lk. 2:10; Phil. 4:1; 1Thess. 2:19, 20; bliss, Mt. 25:21, 23
rows.lsj_entry: chara, he: (chairo): joy, delight; with genitive objecti, joy in or at a thing; opposed to lupe; in concrete sense, a joy, of persons, chara mou [NT]

rows.meaning: to have benefit or joy; optative onaimēn, to receive profit, pleasure, benefit; with a genitive, to have joy of, Phlm. 20
rows.lsj_entry: oninemi [i]: active, profit, benefit, help; middle, have profit or advantage, enjoy help or support, have enjoyment or delight [8th c.BC+]; with genitive, have delight or enjoyment of; aorist optative onaimēn in protestations and wishes [5th c.BC+]

rows.meaning: to be filled with delight, with great joy; to celebrate, praise; usually in the middle in the NT (agalliaŏmai) to exult, rejoice exceedingly; to fill with delight; to desire ardently, Jn. 8:56
rows.lsj_entry: agalliao: late form of agallomai, rejoice exceedingly [NT+3rd c.AD+]; future agalliasŏmai [LXX]; aorist egalliasamen [LXX+NT]
rows.status_note: Luk 10:21 Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit - god_as_subject. Act 2:26 heart glad, tongue rejoiced, flesh dwells in hope - somatic triad explicit.

rows.meaning: to rejoice, be glad, delighted; (as a greeting) Hail!, Greetings!; be joyful, be full of joy; a term of salutation; legō chairein, to greet; an epistolary form, Health! Acts 15:23
rows.lsj_entry: chairo [8th c.BC+]: rejoice, be glad; opposed to lupesthai; with dative, rejoice at; used as greeting at meeting and leavetaking; chairetō, have done with; participle chairōn, joyful; to chairon tēs psychēs [1st c.AD+]
rows.status_note: 74 occurrences include greeting uses (Hail!, Greetings) as well as joy uses. Semantic range: religious joy, relational greeting, epistolary formula, commanded rejoicing.

rows.meaning: to rejoice with, any one, sympathize in joy, Lk. 1:58; 15:6, 9; Phil. 2:17, 18; metaphorically 1Cor. 12:26; to sympathize in the advancement of, congratulate, 1Cor. 13:6
rows.lsj_entry: sunchairo: aorist -echarēn [2nd c.BC+]; rejoice with [5th c.BC+]. II wish one joy, congratulate [4th c.BC+]

_ref.reason: 5 occurrences, 5 verses - small dataset
Luk 1:44 baby leaped for joy in womb - explicit somatic expression of agalliasis
2 occurrences, 2 verses - very thin dataset
Act 2:28 (Psa 16:11 LXX) God makes full of gladness with his presence - God as agent
59 occurrences - dominant NT joy noun; primary anchor of Greek joy semantic field
Covers inner emotional state (Gal 5:22), eschatological bliss (Mt 25:21), cause of joy (Php 4:1), grief-transcending joy (Joh 16:20-22), fruit of Spirit - 4+ domains
NT chara in Philippians and John is commanded, circumstance-independent, Spirit-grounded - meaningful development beyond OT circumstantial rejoicing
Luk 15:7 joy in heaven over one sinner; Luk 15:10 joy before angels - divine joy structurally parallel to human joy in parable
Php 4:4 Rejoice always - commanded; Jam 1:2 Count it all joy - volitional; chara consistently commanded in Paul
1 occurrence (Phlm 20) - hapax legomenon in NT; extremely thin dataset
Luk 10:21 Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit; Joh 8:56 Abraham rejoiced in anticipation of Christ's day
Act 2:26 heart glad, tongue rejoiced, flesh dwells in hope - explicit somatic triad; Luk 1:44 leaping in womb
1Pe 1:6-8 eschatological joy amid trials; Rev 19:7 marriage of the Lamb - eschatological concentration
Jesus rejoices in Holy Spirit (Luk 10:21) while disciples rejoice at same salvific events (Luk 10:20) - divine-human parallel
74 occurrences - highest frequency Greek joy verb; primary verb of NT joy semantic field
Covers inner religious joy (Php 4:4), epistolary greeting (Act 15:23), salvific rejoicing (Luk 19:37), commanded rejoicing (1Th 5:16), empathetic shared joy (Rom 12:15) - 4+ domains
1Th 5:16 Rejoice always; Php 4:4 Rejoice in the Lord always - repeated imperative throughout Paul; strongly volitional
Greek chairo carries greeting sense (Hail!) absent from OT Hebrew parallels; NT develops commanded, Spirit-sustained joy
Rom 12:15 Rejoice with those who rejoice - explicitly relational/sympathetic; sunchairo derivative reinforces relational direction
sunchairo is inherently relational: to rejoice WITH someone; all 7 occurrences involve shared or communal joy directed toward another
7 occurrences, 7 verses - small but complete dataset for a compound verb
sunchairo (sun+chairo) is the Greek parallel to Hebrew shared-joy expressions; bridges OT/NT joy terminology as a compound relational form

rows.verse_text: And you will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth,
For behold, when the sound of your greeting came to my ears, the baby in my womb leaped for joy.
And day by day, attending the temple together and breaking bread in their homes, they received their food with glad and generous hearts,
You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness beyond your companions.
Now to him who is able to keep you from stumbling and to present you blameless before the presence of his glory with great joy,
You have made known to me the paths of life; you will make me full of gladness with your presence.
Yet he did not leave himself without witness, for he did good by giving you rains from heaven and fruitful seasons, satisfying your hearts with food and gladness.
When they saw the star, they rejoiced exceedingly with great joy.
As for what was sown on rocky ground, this is the one who hears the word and immediately receives it with joy,
The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field, which a man found and covered up. Then in his joy he goes and sells all that he has and buys that field.
His master said to him, 'Well done, good and faithful servant. You have been faithful over a little; I will set you over much. Enter into the joy of your master.'
His master said to him, 'Well done, good and faithful servant. You have been faithful over a little; I will set you over much. Enter into the joy of your master.'
So they departed quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, and ran to tell his disciples.
And these are the ones sown on rocky ground: the ones who, when they hear the word, immediately receive it with joy.
And you will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth,
And the angel said to them, 'Fear not, for behold, I bring you good news of great joy that will be for all the people.'
And the ones on the rock are those who, when they hear the word, receive it with joy. But these have no root; they believe for a while, and in time of testing fall away.
The seventy-two returned with joy, saying, 'Lord, even the demons are subject to us in your name!'
Just so, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous persons who need no repentance.
Just so, I tell you, there is joy before the angels of God over one sinner who repents.
And while they still disbelieved for joy and were marveling, he said to them, 'Have you anything here to eat?'
And they worshiped him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy,
The one who has the bride is the bridegroom. The friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly at the bridegroom's voice. Therefore this joy of mine is now complete.
These things I have spoken to you, that my joy may be in you, and that your joy may be full.
Truly, truly, I say to you, you will weep and lament, but the world will rejoice. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.

When a woman is giving birth, she has sorrow because her hour has come, but when she has delivered the baby, she no longer remembers the anguish, for joy that a human being has been born into the world.
So also you have sorrow now, but I will see you again, and your hearts will rejoice, and no one will take your joy from you.
Until now you have asked nothing in my name. Ask, and you will receive, that your joy may be full.
But now I am coming to you, and these things I speak in the world, that they may have my joy fulfilled in themselves.
So there was much joy in that city.
Recognizing Peter's voice, in her joy she did not open the gate but ran in and reported that Peter was standing at the gate.
And the disciples were filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit.
So, being sent on their way by the church, they passed through both Phoenicia and Samaria, describing in detail the conversion of the Gentiles, and brought great joy to all the brothers.
For the kingdom of God is not a matter of eating and drinking but of righteousness and peace and joy in the Holy Spirit.
May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that by the power of the Holy Spirit you may abound in hope.
so that by God's will I may come to you with joy and be refreshed in your company.
Not that we lord it over your faith, but we work with you for your joy, for you stand firm in your faith.
And I wrote as I did, so that when I came I might not suffer pain from those who should have made me rejoice, for I felt sure of all of you, that my joy would be the joy of you all.
I am acting with great boldness toward you; I have great pride in you; I am filled with comfort. In all our affliction, I am overflowing with joy.
Therefore we are comforted. And besides our own comfort, we rejoiced still more at the joy of Titus, because his spirit has been refreshed by you all.
for in a severe test of affliction, their abundance of joy and their extreme poverty have overflowed in a wealth of generosity on their part.
But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
always in every prayer of mine for you all making my prayer with joy,
Convinced of this, I know that I will remain and continue with you all, for your progress and joy in the faith,
complete my joy by being of the same mind, having the same love, being in full accord and of one mind.
So receive him in the Lord with all joy, and honor such men,
Therefore, my brothers, whom I love and long for, my joy and crown, stand firm thus in the Lord, my beloved.
being strengthened with all power, according to his glorious might, for all endurance and patience with joy;
And you became imitators of us and of the Lord, for you received the word in much affliction, with the joy of the Holy Spirit,
For what is our hope or joy or crown of boasting before our Lord Jesus at his coming? Is it not you?
For you are our glory and joy.
For what thanksgiving can we return to God for you, for all the joy that we feel for your sake before our God,
As I remember your tears, I long to see you, that I may be filled with joy.
For I have derived much joy and comfort from your love, my brother, because the hearts of the saints have been refreshed through you.
For you had compassion on those in prison, and you joyfully accepted the plundering of your property, since you knew that you yourselves had a better possession and an abiding one.
looking to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of our faith, who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is seated at the right hand of the throne of God.
For the moment all discipline seems painful rather than pleasant, but later it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who have been trained by it.

Obey your leaders and submit to them, for they are keeping watch over your souls, as those who will have to give an account. Let them do this with joy and not with groaning, for that would be of no advantage to you.
Count it all joy, my brothers, when you meet trials of various kinds,
Be wretched and mourn and weep. Let your laughter be turned to mourning and your joy to gloom.
Though you have not seen him, you love him. Though you do not now see him, you believe in him and rejoice with joy that is inexpressible and filled with glory,
And we are writing these things so that our joy may be complete.
Though I have much to write to you, I would rather not use paper and ink. Instead I hope to come to you and talk face to face, so that our joy may be complete.
I have no greater joy than to hear that my children are walking in the truth.
Yes, brother, I want some benefit from you in the Lord. Refresh my heart in Christ.
Rejoice and be glad, for your reward is great in heaven, for so they persecuted the prophets who were before you.
and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior,

In that same hour he rejoiced in the Holy Spirit and said, 'I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding and revealed them to little children; yes, Father, for such was your gracious will.'
He was a burning and shining lamp, and you were willing to rejoice for a while in his light.
Your father Abraham rejoiced that he would see my day. He saw it and was glad.
therefore my heart was glad, and my tongue rejoiced; my flesh also will dwell in hope.
Then he brought them up into his house and set food before them. And he rejoiced along with his entire household that he had believed in God.
In this you rejoice, though now for a little while, if necessary, you have been grieved by various trials,
Though you have not seen him, you love him. Though you do not now see him, you believe in him and rejoice with joy that is inexpressible and filled with glory,
But rejoice insofar as you share Christ's sufferings, that you may also rejoice and be glad when his glory is revealed.
Let us rejoice and exult and give him the glory, for the marriage of the Lamb has come, and his Bride has made herself ready;
When they saw the star, they rejoiced exceedingly with great joy.
Rejoice and be glad, for your reward is great in heaven, for so they persecuted the prophets who were before you.
And if he finds it, truly, I say to you, he rejoices over it more than over the ninety-nine that never went astray.
And he came up to Jesus at once and said, 'Greetings, Rabbi!' And he kissed him.
and twisting together a crown of thorns, they put it on his head and put a reed in his right hand. And kneeling before him, they mocked him, saying, 'Hail, King of the Jews!'
And behold, Jesus met them and said, 'Greetings!' And they came up and took hold of his feet and worshiped him.
And when they heard it, they were glad and promised to give him money. And he sought an opportunity to betray him.
And they began to salute him, 'Hail, King of the Jews!'
And you will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth,
And he came to her and said, 'Greetings, O favored one, the Lord is with you!'
Rejoice in that day, and leap for joy, for behold, your reward is great in heaven; for so their fathers did to the prophets.
Nevertheless, do not rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.
As he said these things, all his adversaries were put to shame, and all the people rejoiced at all the glorious things that were done by him.
And when he has found it, he lays it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
It was fitting to celebrate and be glad, for this your brother was dead, and is alive; he was lost, and is found.
So he hurried and came down and received him joyfully.

As he was drawing near - already on the way down the Mount of Olives - the whole multitude of his disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works that they had seen,
And they were glad, and agreed to give him money.
When Herod saw Jesus, he was very glad, for he had long desired to see him, because he had heard about him, and he was hoping to see some sign done by him.
The one who has the bride is the bridegroom. The friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly at the bridegroom's voice. Therefore this joy of mine is now complete.
Already the one who reaps is receiving wages and gathering fruit for eternal life, so that sower and reaper may rejoice together.
Your father Abraham rejoiced that he would see my day. He saw it and was glad.
and for your sake I am glad that I was not there, so that you may believe. But let us go to him.
You heard me say to you, 'I am going away, and I will come to you.' If you loved me, you would have rejoiced, because I am going to the Father, for the Father is greater than I.
Truly, truly, I say to you, you will weep and lament, but the world will rejoice. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
So also you have sorrow now, but I will see you again, and your hearts will rejoice, and no one will take your joy from you.
They came up to him, saying, 'Hail, King of the Jews!' and struck him with their hands.
When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his side. Then the disciples were glad when they saw the Lord.
Then they left the presence of the council, rejoicing that they were counted worthy to suffer dishonor for the name.
And when they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord carried Philip away, and the eunuch saw him no more, and went on his way rejoicing.
When he came and saw the grace of God, he was glad, and he exhorted them all to remain faithful to the Lord with steadfast purpose,
And when the Gentiles heard this, they began rejoicing and glorifying the word of the Lord, and as many as were appointed to eternal life believed.
with the following letter: 'The brothers, both the apostles and the elders, to the brothers who are of the Gentiles in Antioch and Syria and Cilicia, greetings.'
And when they had read it, they rejoiced because of its encouragement.
Claudius Lysias, to his Excellency the governor Felix, greetings.
Rejoice in hope, be patient in tribulation, be constant in prayer.
Rejoice with those who rejoice, weep with those who weep.
For your obedience is known to all, so that I rejoice over you, but I want you to be wise as to what is good and innocent as to what is evil.
and those who mourn as though they were not mourning, and those who rejoice as though they were not rejoicing, and those who buy as though they had no goods,
it does not rejoice at wrongdoing, but rejoices with the truth.
I rejoice at the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus, because they have made up for your absence,
And I wrote as I did, so that when I came I might not suffer pain from those who should have made me rejoice, for I felt sure of all of you, that my joy would be the joy of you all.
as sorrowful, yet always rejoicing; as poor, yet making many rich; as having nothing, yet possessing everything.
and not only by his coming but also by the comfort with which he was comforted by you, as he told us of your longing, your mourning, your zeal for me, so that I rejoiced still more.
As it is, I rejoice, not because you were grieved, but because you were grieved into repenting. For you felt a godly grief, so that you suffered no loss through us.
Therefore we are comforted. And besides our own comfort, we rejoiced still more at the joy of Titus, because his spirit has been refreshed by you all.
I rejoice, because I have complete confidence in you.
For we are glad when we are weak and you are strong. Your restoration is what we pray for.
Finally, brothers, rejoice. Aim for restoration, comfort one another, agree with one another, live in peace; and the God of love and peace will be with you.
What then? Only that in every way, whether in pretense or in truth, Christ is proclaimed, and in that I rejoice. Yes, and I will rejoice,
Even if I am to be poured out as a drink offering upon the sacrificial offering of your faith, I am glad and rejoice with you all.
Likewise you also should be glad and rejoice with me.
I am the more eager to send him, therefore, that you may rejoice at seeing him again, and that I may be less anxious.
Finally, my brothers, rejoice in the Lord. To write the same things to you is no trouble to me and is safe for you.
Rejoice in the Lord always; again I will say, rejoice.
I rejoiced in the Lord greatly that now at length you have revived your concern for me. You were indeed concerned for me, but you had no opportunity.
Now I rejoice in my sufferings for your sake, and in my flesh I am filling up what is lacking in Christ's afflictions for the sake of his body, that is, the church,
For though I am absent in body, yet I am with you in spirit, rejoicing to see your good order and the firmness of your faith in Christ.
For what thanksgiving can we return to God for you, for all the joy that we feel for your sake before our God,
Rejoice always,
James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, To the twelve tribes in the Dispersion: Greetings.
But rejoice insofar as you share Christ's sufferings, that you may also rejoice and be glad when his glory is revealed.
I rejoiced greatly to find some of your children walking in the truth, just as we were commanded by the Father.
If anyone comes to you and does not bring this teaching, do not receive him into your house or give him any greeting,
for whoever greets him takes part in his wicked works.
For I rejoiced greatly when the brothers came and testified to your truth, as indeed you are walking in the truth.
and those who dwell on the earth will rejoice over them and make merry and exchange presents, because these two prophets had been a torment to those who dwell on the earth.
Let us rejoice and exult and give him the glory, for the marriage of the Lamb has come, and his Bride has made herself ready;
And her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown great mercy to her, and they rejoiced with her.
And when he comes home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, 'Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep that was lost.'
And when she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, 'Rejoice with me, for I have found the coin that I had lost.'
If one member suffers, all suffer together; if one member is honored, all rejoice together.
it does not rejoice at wrongdoing, but rejoices with the truth.
Even if I am to be poured out as a drink offering upon the sacrificial offering of your faith, I am glad and rejoice with you all.
Likewise you also should be glad and rejoice with me.

rows.description: charmone (G9704) excluded: STEP search returns Strong's number G9704 with no verses attached. No word analysis data available. Excluded from MTI per source file researcher instruction. MTI row id=373 status=excluded.

charopoios (G9706) excluded: STEP search returns Strong's number G9706 with no verses attached. No word analysis data available. Excluded from MTI per source file researcher instruction. MTI row id=374 status=excluded.

agalliama (G6110) excluded: STEP search returns Strong's number G6110 with no verses attached. No word analysis data available. Excluded from MTI per source file researcher instruction. MTI row id=375 status=excluded.

kauchaomai (G2744 to boast) appears in STEP verses header for Part 3 as XREF to registry 123 pride. Analysis held in registry 123. Cross-reference recorded in mti_term_cross_refs (id=73). No wa_term_inventory or wa_verse_records row created for this term in registry 097.

chairo (G5463) stated occurrence count is about 74. Source file verse count extracts 74 verses including epistolary greeting uses (Hail!, Greetings, Acts 15:23, Acts 23:26, Jam 1:1). These greeting uses are part of the full semantic range of chairo and are included per spec. Session B should assess the inner-joy vs greeting distribution.

rows.note: kauchaomai (to boast/rejoice) appears in the STEP joy word list but is owned by registry 123 pride. SHARED_TERM cross-registry link.
chara (joy) and kauchaomai (boasting) share overlapping semantic territory in Paul: Rom 5:2-3, 2Cor 7:4, 1Th 2:19 where joy and boasting appear together as near-synonyms. SEMANTIC_OVERLAP link.
